Convolutional Neural Networks¶

Project: Write an Algorithm for Landmark Classification¶

A simple app¶

In this notebook we build a very simple app that uses our exported model.

Note how we are not importing anything from our source code (we do not use any module from the src directory). This is because the exported model, differently from the model weights, is a standalone serialization of our model and therefore it does not need anything else. You can ship that file to anybody, and as long as they can import torch, they will be able to use your model. This is very important for releasing pytorch models to production.

Test your app¶

Go to a search engine for images (like Google Images) and search for images of some of the landmarks, like the Eiffel Tower, the Golden Gate Bridge, Machu Picchu and so on. Save a few examples locally, then upload them to your app to see how your model behaves!

The app will show the top 5 classes that the model think are most relevant for the picture you have uploaded

In [1]:
from google.colab import drive

In [6]:
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
import torchvision.transforms as T
import torch
import numpy as np
from IPython.display import display, HTML

# External image (not in training/test)
external_image_path = "external_image_3.jpg"

# Load your exported model (transfer learning or scratch)
learn_inf = torch.jit.load("checkpoints/transfer_exported.pt")


# -------------------------------
# 1) Load image and get predictions
# -------------------------------
img = Image.open(external_image_path).convert("RGB")
timg = T.ToTensor()(img).unsqueeze_(0)
softmax = learn_inf(timg).data.cpu().numpy().squeeze()
idxs = np.argsort(softmax)[::-1]

# -------------------------------
# 2) Get top-5 predictions
# -------------------------------
top_preds = []
for i in range(5):
    p = softmax[idxs[i]] * 100  # convert to %
    name = learn_inf.class_names[idxs[i]].split(".", 1)[-1].replace("_", " ")
    top_preds.append((name, p))

# -------------------------------
# 3) Draw predictions on image
# -------------------------------
draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
try:
    font = ImageFont.truetype("arial.ttf", 20)
except:
    font = ImageFont.load_default()

for i, (name, prob) in enumerate(top_preds):
    draw.text((10, 10 + i*25), f"{i+1}. {name} ({prob:.1f}%)", fill="yellow", font=font)

# -------------------------------
# 4) Display in notebook
# -------------------------------
display(HTML("<h3>External Image Predictions</h3>"))
display(img)

# Also display as text
html_preds = "<ol>"
for name, prob in top_preds:
    html_preds += f"<li>{name} ({prob:.1f}%)</li>"
html_preds += "</ol>"
display(HTML(html_preds))

External Image Predictions

  1. Eiffel Tower (89.3%)
  2. Terminal Tower (8.3%)
  3. Gateway of India (1.0%)
  4. Vienna City Hall (0.4%)
  5. Prague Astronomical Clock (0.3%)
